sampling_strategy: From the 190 possible pairs across the 20-value deck, sample 30: (a) every pair where one value lists the other in its internal_tensions field (16 within-domain pairs), and (b) 14 cross-domain pairs targeting documented behavioral tensions from the moral-psychology literature (Haidt MFT loyalty-vs-fairness; cooperation-vs-defection in social dilemmas; whistleblowing as transparency-vs-loyalty; etc.). Pairs are randomized in presentation order per user. Each pair is shown twice across the onboarding sequence to estimate within-user consistency.

pairs.tension_anchor: Tell a hard truth bluntly, or wrap it so it lands gently.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Tell someone an uncomfortable truth, even if it lands badly.
pairs.right_text: Soften the truth so it keeps the relationship intact.

pairs.tension_anchor: Share what people have a right to know, or stay silent when it isn't asked for.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Volunteer information that the other person would want, even if no one asked.
pairs.right_text: Wait to be asked; don't volunteer what isn't strictly needed.

pairs.tension_anchor: Show what you actually feel, or adjust your expression for the context.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Let what you're feeling show, even if it's uncomfortable for the room.
pairs.right_text: Read the room and modulate; what's inside doesn't need to be visible.

pairs.tension_anchor: Volunteer the truth, or stay silent when no one's asking.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Volunteer the truth when you see it; don't wait to be asked.
pairs.right_text: If it isn't asked for and the silence holds, let the silence hold.

pairs.tension_anchor: Give freely from your own, or expect everyone (including yourself) to carry their weight.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Give what you can, even if it means you have less to fall back on.
pairs.right_text: Take care of your own footing before you offer; don't undermine yourself to help.

pairs.tension_anchor: Help those who need it, or give back proportionally to those who gave.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Direct what you have toward who needs it most, regardless of what they put in.
pairs.right_text: Give back to people in proportion to what they gave you.

pairs.tension_anchor: Distribute by clear rule, or give without strict accounting.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Distribute by a principle you could defend to anyone in the group.
pairs.right_text: Give what feels right in the moment; the rules don't always serve the situation.

pairs.tension_anchor: Help those who can't carry their own weight, or expect everyone to.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Lend support to people who genuinely can't manage on their own.
pairs.right_text: Hold the line that people need to carry their own weight; the help can become a trap.

pairs.tension_anchor: Stand with your people, or treat strangers as you'd treat your own.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Stand with the people closest to you, even when an outsider has the better claim.
pairs.right_text: Treat the outsider the way you'd want to be treated; no extra weight to your own.

pairs.tension_anchor: Recognize that close relationships carry weight, or hold them equal to strangers.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Recognize that what you owe family and close friends is different from what you owe a stranger.
pairs.right_text: Hold yourself to the same standard for any person, regardless of relationship.

pairs.tension_anchor: Welcome outsiders into care, or keep a clear line about who's inside.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Welcome the outsider in; expand the circle of care.
pairs.right_text: Keep the circle clear; you can't be responsible to everyone.

pairs.tension_anchor: Protect your existing people, or welcome new ones into care.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Tend to the people who are already in your life before extending to new ones.
pairs.right_text: Make room for outsiders, even if it means less attention to your existing circle.

pairs.tension_anchor: Extend the benefit of the doubt, or stay protected against being used.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Default to trust; assume good intent until proven otherwise.
pairs.right_text: Stay alert; people often act in their own interest, and you'll regret missing it.

pairs.tension_anchor: Work with others, or act on your own judgment.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Bring others in; the result is better when it's shared, even if slower.
pairs.right_text: Act on your own judgment; consensus dilutes.

pairs.tension_anchor: Re-open cooperation after someone let you down, or stay protected.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Re-open the door after someone has let you down; people change.
pairs.right_text: Once someone has shown what they'll do, take it seriously and protect yourself.

pairs.tension_anchor: Engage cooperatively, or protect against being used.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Engage with the group; the relationship matters more than the worst-case.
pairs.right_text: Watch for the pattern of being the one who carries the cost; protect yourself.

pairs.tension_anchor: Tell the uncomfortable truth, or protect your people. Classic Haidt MFT tension; the happy-hour scenario.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Tell someone an uncomfortable truth that affects them, even if it costs your closest people.
pairs.right_text: Protect your closest people, even if it means leaving an uncomfortable truth unsaid.

pairs.tension_anchor: Whistleblower's dilemma: surface what should be known, or stay loyal to those affected.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Surface something that should be known, even though it will hurt people you're close to.
pairs.right_text: Stay loyal to the people who'd be hurt; what's not asked doesn't have to be answered.

pairs.tension_anchor: Give freely, or protect against being used.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Give what you can; the risk of being used is part of what generosity costs.
pairs.right_text: Pay attention to who's asking and why; not everyone deserves your help.

pairs.tension_anchor: Give to strangers without expecting return, or limit giving to those who give back.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Help strangers who can't return the favor; that's part of what helping is.
pairs.right_text: Give back to people in proportion to what they put in; charity is for charity, not for everyone.

pairs.tension_anchor: Distribute by rule, or recognize that close relationships have special claim.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Treat the situation by clear principle, even when it disadvantages your own.
pairs.right_text: Recognize that your own have a stronger claim on what you can give.

pairs.tension_anchor: Treat by clear rule, or favor your people.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Apply the same standard to your closest people that you'd apply to anyone else.
pairs.right_text: Give your people a little more benefit of the doubt; loyalty has weight that rules don't capture.

pairs.tension_anchor: Help anyone in genuine need, or keep clear about who's in your circle.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Respond to need wherever you see it; the circle expands when it has to.
pairs.right_text: Stay clear about who you're responsible to; otherwise the demands have no end.

pairs.tension_anchor: Go along with the group, or voice the dissent.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Get behind the group's decision once it's made; collective action requires it.
pairs.right_text: Say what you actually think, even when the group is moving the other way.

pairs.tension_anchor: Welcome outsiders, or be cautious about who you let in.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Welcome the newcomer; the risk of being taken advantage of is part of the cost of welcome.
pairs.right_text: Be cautious about who you let in; not every stranger is what they appear.

pairs.tension_anchor: Believe what someone tells you, or push back when something doesn't ring true.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Take what people say at face value; the alternative corrodes the relationship.
pairs.right_text: Press when something doesn't quite add up; the truth matters more than the comfort.

pairs.tension_anchor: Act on your own judgment, or defer to your people.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Make the call you think is right, even when your people prefer a different one.
pairs.right_text: Defer to your people; loyalty isn't loyalty if you only stay when you agree.

pairs.tension_anchor: Handle it yourself, or ask for help.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Take care of it yourself; asking for help imposes a cost on others.
pairs.right_text: Bring others in; getting help is part of how the relationship gets built.

pairs.tension_anchor: Move past what happened, or name it explicitly.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Let it go; rehashing isn't always useful, and the relationship is more than the offense.
pairs.right_text: Name what happened; pretending it didn't will distort everything after it.

pairs.tension_anchor: Show what you actually feel, or protect your people by hiding it.
pairs.prompt: When you can't fully have both:
pairs.left_text: Let your honest reaction show, even when it makes things harder for your people.
pairs.right_text: Hold what you're feeling; sometimes loyalty looks like not showing it.
